description: This PRD addresses the remaining gaps in the Laravel starter kit to bring it to production-ready, ship-quality completeness. Seven workstreams close the remaining gaps: MCP route verification, CI enforcement of code style/static analysis, example durable workflows, global search UI backed by Scout, billing webhook test coverage, Paddle/Lemon Squeezy documentation, and a single-page auth feature checklist.

userStories.title: Verify MCP route registration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to confirm that the MCP endpoint at /mcp/api is reachable so that AI assistants can connect to the application's MCP server.
acceptanceCriteria: routes/ai.php is loaded (either by the laravel/mcp package auto-discovery or explicit registration in bootstrap/app.php)
php artisan route:list --path=mcp shows the MCP route
A feature test confirms POST /mcp/api returns a valid MCP response when authenticated via Sanctum
If manual registration is needed, add it to bootstrap/app.php with a comment explaining why
Documentation updated in docs/developer/backend/ if any changes were made
vendor/bin/pint --dirty --format agent passes
php artisan test --compact --filter=Mcp passes

userStories.title: Add Pint code style check to CI
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CI to enforce code style via Laravel Pint so that style regressions are caught before merge.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/tests.yml includes a step that runs vendor/bin/pint --test
The step runs before the test suite (fail fast on style)
If Pint fails, the workflow fails with a clear error message
The step name is descriptive (e.g. "Check code style (Pint)")
Verified by pushing a deliberately mis-styled file and confirming CI catches it

userStories.title: Add Rector dry-run to CI (optional but recommended)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CI to run Rector in dry-run mode so that automated refactoring regressions are detected.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/tests.yml includes a step that runs vendor/bin/rector --dry-run
The step runs after Pint and before tests
If Rector finds changes, the workflow fails with a clear diff
The step name is descriptive (e.g. "Check refactoring rules (Rector)")
Rector config (rector.php) is confirmed to exist and be valid

userStories.title: Create user onboarding workflow
userStories.description: As a developer using this starter kit, I want a reference onboarding workflow so I can understand how to build durable workflows with laravel-workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: app/Workflows/OnboardingWorkflow.php created extending Workflow
Activities: SendWelcomeEmailActivity, SetupDefaultOrganizationActivity, SendOnboardingCompleteNotificationActivity
Each activity class lives in app/Workflows/Activities/
Workflow is triggerable via an Artisan command: php artisan workflow:onboarding {userId}
Workflow appears in Waterline UI at /waterline when run
Feature test verifies the workflow completes successfully with all activities executed
vendor/bin/pint --dirty --format agent passes
Documentation created at docs/developer/backend/durable-workflow.md (or updated if exists) with usage examples

userStories.title: Create approval workflow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a reference approval workflow demonstrating submit/review/approve/reject patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: app/Workflows/ApprovalWorkflow.php created extending Workflow
Activities: SubmitForReviewActivity, NotifyReviewerActivity, ProcessDecisionActivity
Workflow accepts a decision signal (approve/reject) to proceed
Rejection loops back or terminates with appropriate status
Approval completes the workflow with a success status
Workflow is triggerable via Artisan command: php artisan workflow:approval {modelType} {modelId}
Feature test verifies both approve and reject paths
vendor/bin/pint --dirty --format agent passes

userStories.title: Create AI pipeline workflow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a reference AI pipeline workflow demonstrating how to orchestrate multi-step AI tasks using laravel-workflow and laravel/ai.
acceptanceCriteria: app/Workflows/AiPipelineWorkflow.php created extending Workflow
Activities: GenerateContentActivity, ReviewContentActivity, PublishContentActivity
GenerateContentActivity uses laravel/ai SDK to generate text content
ReviewContentActivity uses laravel/ai to review/score the generated content
PublishContentActivity stores the final result (e.g. to a model or log)
Workflow is triggerable via Artisan command: php artisan workflow:ai-pipeline {prompt}
Graceful failure handling: if AI generation fails, workflow retries or fails with a clear status
Feature test verifies the pipeline completes (using mocked AI responses)
vendor/bin/pint --dirty --format agent passes

userStories.title: Document Typesense setup for production
userStories.description: As a developer deploying to production, I need clear documentation on how to configure Scout with Typesense so full-text search works beyond the default collection driver.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/developer/backend/scout-typesense.md updated (or created) with: Required environment variables (SCOUT_DRIVER=typesense, TYPESENSE_API_KEY, TYPESENSE_HOST, TYPESENSE_PORT)
Herd-specific setup instructions (TYPESENSE_API_KEY=LARAVEL-HERD, localhost)
Production Typesense Cloud setup instructions
How to add new searchable models (trait, toSearchableArray(), config/scout.php schema)
How to index existing data (php artisan scout:import)
List of currently searchable models: User, Post, HelpArticle, ChangelogEntry
Documentation manifest updated

userStories.title: Create global search API endpoint
userStories.description: As the frontend search component, I need a backend API endpoint that searches across all searchable models and returns unified results.
acceptanceCriteria: SearchController created with a single __invoke method
Route: GET /search (authenticated, under tenant middleware) accepting q (query string) and optional type filter
Searches across User, Post, HelpArticle, ChangelogEntry (respecting feature flags for blog, changelog, help)
Results respect organization scope (tenant context)
Results respect visibility and permissions (user can only see what they have access to)
Returns JSON with grouped results: { users: [...], posts: [...], help_articles: [...], changelog_entries: [...] }
Each result includes: id, title (or name), subtitle (secondary text), url (link to the item), type
Maximum 5 results per category, 20 total
Feature test covers: authenticated search, empty query, filtered by type, respects tenant scope
vendor/bin/pint --dirty --format agent passes

userStories.title: Build global search command palette UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to press Cmd+K (or click the search icon in the header) to open a search dialog that searches across the entire application so I can quickly find what I need.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing command-dialog.tsx extended (or replaced) with a search-enabled version
Search input at top of dialog with debounced (300ms) queries to the /search endpoint
Results grouped by category (Users, Posts, Help Articles, Changelog Entries) with icons per category
Each result shows title, subtitle, and a type badge
Clicking a result navigates to that item's page via router.visit()
Arrow keys navigate results, Enter selects, Escape closes
Empty state: shows navigation shortcuts (existing command palette items) when no query entered
Loading state: shows skeleton/spinner while fetching
No results state: shows "No results found for {query}"
Search button in app-header.tsx (currently a placeholder) triggers the dialog
Keyboard shortcut Cmd+K / Ctrl+K opens the dialog (already wired)
data-pan="global-search" added to the search trigger button; Pan analytics name registered in AppServiceProvider::configurePan()
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit or equivalent)

userStories.title: Add search result highlighting
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my search term highlighted in the results so I can quickly identify why each result matched.
acceptanceCriteria: Matched text in result titles and subtitles is visually highlighted (bold or background color)
Highlighting is case-insensitive
Special characters in the search query are escaped to prevent XSS
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Stripe webhook integration tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests that verify Stripe webhook handling so that subscription and invoice events are processed correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file: tests/Feature/Billing/StripeWebhookTest.php
Tests cover: customer.subscription.created, customer.subscription.updated, customer.subscription.deleted, invoice.paid, invoice.payment_failed
Each test POSTs a realistic payload to /webhooks/stripe
Asserts correct database side effects (subscription created/updated/cancelled, invoice recorded)
Asserts webhook is logged to WebhookLog table
Tests signature validation (rejects unsigned payloads when signing secret is configured)
vendor/bin/pint --dirty --format agent passes
php artisan test --compact --filter=StripeWebhook passes

userStories.title: Add Paddle webhook integration tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests that verify Paddle webhook handling so that transaction and subscription events are processed correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file: tests/Feature/Billing/PaddleWebhookTest.php
Tests cover the events handled by PaddleWebhookController
Each test POSTs a realistic payload to /webhooks/paddle
Asserts correct database side effects
Asserts webhook is logged to WebhookLog table
vendor/bin/pint --dirty --format agent passes
php artisan test --compact --filter=PaddleWebhook passes

userStories.title: Add Lemon Squeezy webhook integration tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests that verify Lemon Squeezy webhook handling so that credit purchases via one-time orders are processed correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file: tests/Feature/Billing/LemonSqueezyWebhookTest.php
Tests cover: order_created event dispatching OrderCreated and triggering AddCreditsFromLemonSqueezyOrder listener
Test verifies credits are added to the correct organization based on custom metadata (billable_type, billable_id, credits, credit_pack_id)
Test verifies fallback credit calculation: totalCents / centsPerCredit when no explicit credits in metadata
Test verifies order_refunded event handling (if implemented)
Test verifies signature validation rejection (when signing secret is set)
vendor/bin/pint --dirty --format agent passes
php artisan test --compact --filter=LemonSqueezy passes

userStories.title: Add AddCreditsFromLemonSqueezyOrder listener unit test
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a unit test for the AddCreditsFromLemonSqueezyOrder listener so the credit calculation logic is verified in isolation.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file: tests/Unit/Listeners/AddCreditsFromLemonSqueezyOrderTest.php
Tests: credits from explicit metadata, credits from amount calculation, missing organization handling, zero-amount handling
vendor/bin/pint --dirty --format agent passes
php artisan test --compact --filter=AddCreditsFromLemonSqueezyOrder passes

userStories.title: Document supported billing providers
userStories.description: As a developer setting up billing, I need a clear reference documenting which billing providers are supported, what each one handles, and how to configure them.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/developer/backend/billing-providers.md created (or billing-and-tenancy.md updated) with: Overview stating this starter supports Stripe (subscriptions + invoices), Paddle (subscriptions + transactions), and Lemon Squeezy (one-time credit purchases)
Provider comparison table: what each handles (subscriptions vs one-time), webhook URLs, required env vars
Stripe setup: env vars, webhook URL (/webhooks/stripe), handled events
Paddle setup: env vars, webhook URL (/webhooks/paddle), handled events, note that it's optional
Lemon Squeezy setup: env vars, webhook URL (/lemon-squeezy/webhook — vendor-managed), handled events, credit calculation logic
How to disable a provider you don't need (env vars, removing routes)
Per-org override instructions (reference organization_settings and ApplyOrganizationSettings middleware)
Documentation manifest updated

userStories.title: Create auth feature checklist documentation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a single-page reference listing every authentication feature, its current status, and how to toggle it so I can quickly configure auth for my needs.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/developer/backend/auth/auth-features-checklist.md created with: Table listing every auth feature: Login, Registration, Password Reset, Email Verification, Two-Factor Authentication (2FA), Password Confirmation, Profile Update
For each feature: current status (enabled/disabled), where it's configured (config/fortify.php, Pennant feature flag, middleware), how to enable/disable
Note that registration is controlled by a Pennant feature flag (not Fortify's Features::registration())
Note that only 2FA is enabled in config/fortify.php currently
Guard configuration: web guard, email as username field, lowercase usernames enabled
Reference to the Inertia React pages that implement each auth flow
Reference to relevant Fortify Actions in app/Actions/Fortify/
Documentation manifest updated

userStories.title: Add inline comments to config/fortify.php
userStories.description: As a developer reading the Fortify config, I want clear inline comments explaining each feature toggle and its implications.
acceptanceCriteria: config/fortify.php features section has a comment block listing all available Fortify features and their current on/off status
Each commented-out feature has a note explaining why it's disabled (e.g. "Registration is managed by Pennant feature flag instead")
vendor/bin/pint --dirty --format agent passes

userStories.title: Verify all changes pass existing test suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to confirm that all additions from this PRD do not break any existing tests.
acceptanceCriteria: composer test:full passes with no regressions
vendor/bin/pint --test passes
vendor/bin/rector --dry-run passes (if Rector is configured)
php artisan docs:sync --check passes
All new test files pass individually
CI workflow (if modified) runs successfully on a test push
